note: AI Feynman (Udrescu & Tegmark, Science Advances 6:eaay2631, 2020; arXiv:1905.11481v2, physics.comp-ph) is a recursive symbolic-regression method that takes a data table {x1..xn, y} for an unknown physics function f and searches for a matching symbolic expression. Both manuscript claims are ACCURATE. C071: correctly listed under the physics domain -- the 100+20 benchmark equations are drawn from the Feynman Lectures on Physics and famous physics texts (Goldstein/Jackson/Weinberg/Schwartz). C072 (AI Feynman half): 'fit equations to data with deterministic search' is accurate -- the core symbolic search is the brute-force module that deterministically enumerates all syntactically-valid symbol strings by increasing complexity (reverse Polish notation), aided by dimensional analysis (null-space linear algebra) and polynomial fitting (linear systems), all deterministic; the neural network is used only as an interpolator to detect symmetry/separability so variables can be eliminated, not as the search itself, and the paper explicitly contrasts this deterministic approach with the stochastic genetic algorithm Eureqa. 'Validation is empirical' is accurate -- modules declare success on r.m.s. fit-error thresholds, with a final symbolic identity check (simplify(f'-f)=0). 'No memory transfer across problems: each task starts fresh' is accurate -- a fresh neural network is trained per mystery and each transformed sub-problem is passed to 'a fresh instantiation of the full AI Feynman algorithm'; nothing learned on one equation carries to another (the only cross-problem element is manual hyperparameter tuning: 100 basic = train set, 20 bonus = held-out test set).

C071.evidence: Manuscript L176 lists 'physics~\citep{udrescu2020afeynman,cranmer2020symbolic}' in the seven-field domain map. AI Feynman is unambiguously a physics paper: title 'AI Feynman: a Physics-Inspired Method for Symbolic Regression'; arXiv category physics.comp-ph; the benchmark is 100 equations 'from the seminal Feynman Lectures on Physics [34-36]... covering classical mechanics, electromagnetism and quantum mechanics' plus 20 bonus equations 'extracted from other seminal physics books: Classical Mechanics by Goldstein, Classical electrodynamics by Jackson, Gravitation and Cosmology by Weinberg, Quantum Field Theory and the Standard Model by Schwartz' (paper Sec III.A). Domain attribution is correct.

C072.evidence: Manuscript L440 (Appendix, Physics paragraph): 'AI Feynman~\citep{udrescu2020afeynman} and symbolic-model discovery~\citep{cranmer2020symbolic} fit equations to data with deterministic search. Validation is empirical and of medium reliability, but the systems carry no memory transfer across problems: each task starts fresh.' AI Feynman half fully supported. (1) 'fit equations to data with deterministic search': the task is symbolic regression over a data table {x1..xn,y} for unknown f (paper Sec I); the core symbolic search is the brute-force module which 'simply tries all possible symbolic expressions within some class, in order of increasing complexity' -- deterministic exhaustive enumeration of reverse-Polish symbol strings (Sec II.D, Table I); dimensional analysis (null-space of the units matrix, Eq.1) and polynomial fit (solving linear systems for coefficients, degrees 0..4, Sec II.C) are also deterministic. The neural network (Sec II.E) is only an interpolator used to TEST for translational/scaling symmetry (Alg.1) and additive/multiplicative separability (Alg.2), enabling variable elimination -- it is not the search. The paper repeatedly contrasts its deterministic progress ('every time [variable reduction] occurs, it is virtually guaranteed to be a step in the right direction') against the stochastic genetic algorithm Eureqa which 'make[s] progress... by finding successively better approximations... risking getting stuck in local optima' (Sec III.B). (2) 'Validation is empirical': modules declare success when r.m.s. fit error drops below tolerances (brute force 1e-5, polyfit 1e-4; Table II), i.e. empirical fit to data, with a final symbolic-identity answer-check (simplify(f'-f)='0' in Mathematica/sympy; Sec III.A). (3) 'no memory transfer across problems: each task starts fresh': for each mystery a fresh NN is trained on 100,000 freshly generated data points (Sec II.E.1), and every simplified sub-problem is 'iteratively passed into a fresh instantiation of our full AI Feynman symbolic regression algorithm' (Sec II.E.2, II.E.3). No learned weights or patterns transfer between distinct mysteries. Faithful characterization.
C072.fix: None needed. (Optional, non-blocking) 'medium reliability' is the manuscript's own domain coding rather than a stated figure in the paper; it is a reasonable paraphrase given the paper flags 'de facto noise' from imperfect NN fitting (~1e-3 to 1e-5) and noise-robustness limits, so no change required.

reverse_findings.fact: The paper is internally inconsistent about the Eureqa baseline on the 100 basic Feynman equations: the Abstract and Sec III.B both state Eureqa 'solved 71%' of the 100 basic mysteries, while Sec IV.A 'Key findings' states Eureqa 'discovered 68% of the Feynman equations'. AI Feynman's own figure (100%) is consistent throughout, as is the bonus-set comparison (Eureqa 15%, AI Feynman 90%).
reverse_findings.loc: paper Abstract / Sec III.B (71%) vs Sec IV.A (68%)
reverse_findings.mismatch: No conflict with C072 or C071 -- the manuscript does not cite either Eureqa percentage, so the source's internal inconsistency does not affect the accuracy of the manuscript's claims. Logged only as a source-quality caveat should any future manuscript version quote the AI-Feynman-vs-Eureqa headline number.

reverse_findings.fact: The characterization 'deterministic search' applies to the symbolic search itself (brute-force enumeration + dimensional analysis + polynomial fit), but the AI Feynman pipeline also contains a stochastic component: per-mystery neural-network training (random init, Adam optimizer). That NN is used only to detect symmetry/separability (variable elimination), not to search the space of symbolic expressions.
reverse_findings.loc: paper Sec II.E.1 (NN training) vs Sec II.D (brute-force search)
reverse_findings.mismatch: Minor nuance, not an error. The claim scopes 'deterministic search' to the search strategy, which is accurate and is the exact property the manuscript uses to contrast with genetic/stochastic methods (Eureqa). A maximally-precise reading is that the overall pipeline embeds one stochastic sub-step (NN fitting) whose imperfections the paper itself notes introduce 'de facto noise'. Does not weaken C072.
